scene_summary: A vast matte vermilion field fills the frame, borrowing the supplied album-cover evidence: hot red ground, imperfect hand-drawn pale arcs, sparse inscription, and a sun-worn science-fiction western mood. In the lower half, one dominant form: a half-dome astrolabe of nested chalk-white and ochre arcs, like an observatory instrument drawn directly onto desert pigment. Two secondary forms balance it only lightly: a low smoked-obsidian horizon bar beneath the arcs and a small brushed-aluminum lens moon near the upper right. Keep more than half the image empty red air. Add tiny quiet anchors as etched labels, pinholes, nicks, and constellation ticks along the arcs and horizon: Flower Music, Shu Qin, Surf Noir, Into the Light, Brise, Open World, Jam, Galaxy DNA. No room, no signal board, no archive wall. The mood is nocturnal, optical, dry, still, and reverent.
